programmes.show_description: Sketch tackles Nick's measuring stick and covers Fran's naughty nickname. Glen helps heathen Bob get an honour inking. Jay removes Lee's charming chat-up line.
Five celebrities enter Greg Davies' lair to begin a bitter battle for glory. Alex Horne invigilates while trying to avoid a barrage of fruit.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Kerry Godliman take on Jon Richardson and Joe Wilkinson. With Susie Dent, Rachel Riley and Dictionary Corner guest Mr Swallow, aka Nick Mohammed.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
In Kent, Katie and 6' 7'' Dan serve portions suited to Dan's giant appetite. Simon and Jacqui flummox their guests with a French menu. And can Paul and Claire dance their way to the grand?
In Dumfries, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien get to work on Maggie's farm, which boasts an extraordinary collection of memorabilia.
The One at a Music Festival: Jake wants the ultimate wedding knees-up, but bride-to-be Rosie dreams of a more intimate affair. Will this festival wedding prove to be a headliner?
The One with the Wannabe King: Harry wants to be king for the day. But bride Charlotte wants something low key. Will this be Harry's crowning moment or will it be 'off with his head'?
The One in an Old Mine: It's high noon for impulsive groom Steve as he plans a cliff-hanger of a Wild West wedding for Leona. Will Steve's nuptials on a bridge prove a swinging success?
The One with the Wight Wedding: Groom Liam wants a wedding day that celebrates his homeland, the Isle of Wight. Bride Kandi wants a more standard day. Will it be all-white or go all wrong?
...in the Snow: Laid-back Liam plans to wow his bride with a wintry white wedding. But 2000 miles away in Portugal, bride-to-be Georgia is dreaming of sun, sand and sangria.
Paul Damico, president of Moe's Southwest Grill, one of the world's fastest-growing restaurant chains, goes undercover to find out how efficient his organisation is.
Sara Bittorf, the chief brand officer of Boston Market - whose 470 restaurants serve home-style comfort food - goes undercover to work alongside the chain's employees.
J Willy's Bar & Grille: Gordon's got his work cut out at J Willy's in Indiana - most of the food is from a can or the freezer, and they're a million dollars in debt.
Fiesta Sunrise: A cockroach-infested kitchen means that the customers have to leave for their own safety at Fiesta Sunrise, a Mexican restaurant in West Nyack.
Sante La Brea: Gordon visits Sante La Brea, a health food restaurant in Hollywood. But there's nothing glamorous about this eatery as owner Dean faces financial ruin.
Cafe 36: Gordon Ramsay visits Cafe 36 in Chicago. Husband-and-wife team Terry and Carol are stuck in the past and their head chef's lax attitude is driving away customers.
Casa Roma: Gordon visits Casa Roma, the oldest restaurant in Lancaster, California. Although the bar is a success, the restaurant is failing and has had 20 chefs in two years.
It's Jon Richardson and Asim Chaudhry v Joe Wilkinson and Roisin Conaty. Lucy Pearman + Susie Dent = Dictionary Corner. Jimmy hosts. Rachel's on letters and numbers.
Da-da, da-da, da-da-da-da, DOOF! Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and Dan Tiernan take on Richard Ayoade and Katherine Ryan. With John Kearns.
...at a Dog: Greg's hugely successful and acclaimed debut stand-up show from 2011, which was a huge hit at the Edinburgh Festival and went on a sell-out, UK-wide tour.
Moo-Ma and Moo-Pa: Manny's parents come to stay - they're a nice couple with one or two slightly annoying habits. Which is why Bernard wants rid of them, preferably yesterday.
A Little Flutter: Manny puts a bet on the Grand National for Bernard, who claims not to be interested in gambling but immediately becomes completely addicted to it.
An extended edition of the show with host Jimmy Carr, team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson and guests Micky Flanagan, Hari Kondabolu, Tess Daly and Krishnan Guru-Murthy.
With Jimmy Carr, team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson and guests Heston Blumenthal, double Paralympic gold medallist Hannah Cockroft, Josh Widdicombe and Katherine Ryan.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
The One with the Thai Stag Do: Cheeky chappie Ian travels to Thailand to get three suits made, picks up a wedding dress for his bride Jay, and has an unforgettable stag do.
The One with the Cheap Dress: Scott spends less than £100 for a wedding dress and £1800 on life-size plastic animals.
The Remote Island One: Dave wants to whisk his water phobic bride-to-be Rosie off to a remote island wedding. But it's not just any island...
CEO Andy Wirth takes on a new identity to work incognito among his own employees at a gigantic ski resort near Lake Tahoe.
Fatburger, based in Los Angeles, is known as the 'last great hamburger stand'. CEO Andy Wiederhorn dons a disguise to go undercover in his own organisation.
In Belfast, Leeann doesn't enjoy Gemma and Jim's health-focused food, or even her own takeaway-inspired menu. Can Matt and Paul impress her with their posh nosh and unique dance routines?
In Belfast, newly-wed am-dram lovers Mel and Richard take on rockers Joanne and Jim and confident Cherith and David, in a week featuring a love-themed menu and spicy banter.
In Cardiff, Jackie and Oasis tribute singer Degsy pay homage to Manchester. Jo and Vikki make their Indian menu from scratch. And will critical Chris and Bex's bickering blow their chances?
Three couples compete in south Wales. Nigel and Nikki serve up a taste of South Africa, and fine diners Darren and Simon hire a butler - a step too far for down-to-earth Neil and Carly.
Gordon Returns: Gordon Ramsay revisits some of the restaurants he set out to save, including The Mixing Bowl, The Olde Stone Mill, Peter's and Campania. Have things improved?
Hot Potato Café: The Hot Potato Café in Philadelphia has never made a profit. When the chips are down, can Gordon help save the restaurant?
Flamangos: Gordon visits Flamangos in rural New Jersey, where mother and daughter Adele and Cheryl are stuck in their tropical ways. But their restaurant is far from paradise.
Bazzini: Award-winning chef Paul runs Italian joint Bazzini in New Jersey, but didn't realise how difficult running a restaurant would be. Can Gordon turn it around?
Mojito: Gordon Ramsay visits a Cuban café in Brooklyn, where owners Marcelo and Kata fight over everything, driving away customers with their high-volume bickering.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and Rosie Jones take on Joe Wilkinson and Phil Wang. Dr John Cooper Clarke joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts a festive edition of the comedy panel show. Rob Beckett, Tom Allen and Liam Charles take on Katherine Ryan, Scarlett Moffatt and Maisie Adam.

programmes.show_name: Jordan Gray: Is It a Bird?
programmes.show_description: Jordan brings her comedy to TV for the first time. Shot in her Essex home-town theatre, this stand-up special is a whirlwind of music and comedy covering everything from politics to Batman.
